label: Caches, APIs & tool interfaces

node.label: Caches, APIs & tool interfaces
node.definition: Reusable state, external functions, services, and interfaces extend computation beyond model parameters.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Caches, APIs & tool interfaces is to connect Algorithms & data structures, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations to downstream capabilities including KV cache & recurrent inference state, External databases, APIs & live tools, Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes.
examples: substrate for KV cache & recurrent inference state
substrate for External databases, APIs & live tools
substrate for Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.

dependencies.rationale: Caches, APIs & tool interfaces uses Algorithms & data structures as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Caches, APIs & tool interfaces uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Caches, APIs & tool interfaces uses Databases & information retrieval as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Caches, APIs & tool interfaces uses Distributed systems & networking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
